- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- vercel-react-best-practices: React and Next.js performance optimization guidelines from Vercel Engineering. This skill should be used when writing, reviewing, or refactoring React/Next.js code to ensure optimal performance patterns. Triggers on tasks involving React components, Next.js pages, data fetching, bundle optimization, or performance improvements.
- web-design-guidelines: Review UI code for Web Interface Guidelines compliance. Use when asked to "review my UI", "check accessibility", "audit design", "review UX", or "check my site against best practices".
- calcom-api: Interact with the Cal.diy API v2 to manage scheduling, bookings, event types, availability, and calendars. Use this skill when building integrations that need to create or manage bookings, check availability, configure event types, or sync calendars with Cal.diy's scheduling infrastructure.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

/workspace/docker-compose.alloy.yaml
# Alloy dev-env Docker Compose for Cal.diy
#
# Boots the smallest set of services needed to render the Cal.diy
# Next.js frontend at http://localhost:8080 inside the Alloy sandbox.
#
# Services:
#   - postgres: Postgres 18 backing store on host's :5432
#   - web:      @calcom/web Next.js dev server on host's :8080
#
# All services use host networking so they can talk to each other via
# 127.0.0.1 the same way the Alloy proxy reaches the frontend.

services:
  postgres:
    image: postgres:18
    network_mode: host
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: postgres
      POSTGRES_DB: calendso
      POSTGRES_HOST_AUTH_METHOD: trust
    volumes:
      - alloy-postgres-data:/var/lib/postgresql
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U postgres -d calendso -h 127.0.0.1 -p 5432"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 60
    restart: unless-stopped

  web:
    image: node:20-bookworm
    working_dir: /workspace
    network_mode: host
    volumes:
      - .:/workspace
      - alloy-yarn-cache:/usr/local/share/.cache/yarn
    environment:
      # --- Database ---
      DATABASE_URL: "postgresql://postgres@127.0.0.1:5432/calendso"
      DATABASE_DIRECT_URL: "postgresql://postgres@127.0.0.1:5432/calendso"
      PGSSLMODE: ""

      # --- Public URLs ---
      NEXT_PUBLIC_WEBAPP_URL: "http://localhost:8080"
      NEXT_PUBLIC_WEBSITE_URL: "http://localhost:8080"
      NEXT_PUBLIC_API_V2_URL: ""
      NEXT_PUBLIC_EMBED_LIB_URL: "http://localhost:8080/embed/embed.js"
      ALLOWED_HOSTNAMES: '"localhost:8080","127.0.0.1:8080","localhost","127.0.0.1"'

      # --- NextAuth ---
      NEXTAUTH_URL: "http://localhost:8080"
      NEXTAUTH_SECRET: "alloy_dev_only_nextauth_secret_change_me_xxxxx"
      NEXTAUTH_COOKIE_DOMAIN: ""
      # The Alloy preview serves the app over HTTPS inside a cross-site iframe.
      # Browsers won't send `SameSite=Lax` cookies on subresource requests from
      # cross-site iframes, so we force `SameSite=None; Secure` for all NextAuth
      # cookies. Safe here because the proxy terminates HTTPS.
      NEXTAUTH_FORCE_SECURE_COOKIES: "true"

      # --- Cal.com required encryption key (must be 24 bytes base64) ---
      CALENDSO_ENCRYPTION_KEY: "ZGV2X2FsbG95X2VuY3J5cHRpb25fZGV2X3Nl"

      # --- Telemetry / branding ---
      CALCOM_TELEMETRY_DISABLED: "1"
      NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_NAME: "Cal.diy"
      NEXT_PUBLIC_COMPANY_NAME: "Cal.com, Inc."
      NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPPORT_MAIL_ADDRESS: "help@cal.diy"

      # --- Email (no-op in dev) ---
      EMAIL_FROM: "notifications@cal.diy"
      EMAIL_FROM_NAME: "Cal.diy"
      EMAIL_SERVER_HOST: "127.0.0.1"
      EMAIL_SERVER_PORT: "1025"

      # --- Cron / misc ---
      CRON_API_KEY: "alloy_dev_cron_key"
      CRON_ENABLE_APP_SYNC: "false"
      API_KEY_PREFIX: "cal_"

      # --- Next.js dev server port ---
      PORT: "8080"
      HOST: "0.0.0.0"
      HOSTNAME: "0.0.0.0"

      # --- Memory / performance ---
      NODE_OPTIONS: "--max-old-space-size=8192"
      NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED: "1"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    command:
      - bash
      - -lc
      - |
        set -euo pipefail
        echo "[alloy] enabling corepack / yarn 4..."
        corepack enable
        corepack prepare yarn@4.12.0 --activate

        if [ ! -d node_modules ] || [ ! -d apps/web/node_modules ]; then
          echo "[alloy] running yarn install (first boot — may take a while)..."
          yarn install --inline-builds
        else
          echo "[alloy] node_modules present — skipping yarn install"
        fi

        echo "[alloy] applying prisma migrations..."
        yarn workspace @calcom/prisma prisma migrate deploy

        echo "[alloy] seeding app store + basic data (best effort)..."
        yarn workspace @calcom/prisma seed-app-store || echo "[alloy] seed-app-store failed (non-fatal)"
        yarn workspace @calcom/prisma seed-basic || echo "[alloy] seed-basic failed (non-fatal)"

        echo "[alloy] starting Next.js dev server on :8080..."
        cd apps/web
        exec yarn dev --port 8080 --hostname 0.0.0.0
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  alloy-postgres-data:
  alloy-yarn-cache:

content.thinking:
So the redirect from `/` back to `/auth/login` is happening even though the session is valid—the API confirmed the user is logged in. The session token is HttpOnly so it won't show in `document.cookie`, but the fetch with credentials included did return the authenticated user, so something in the home page logic is still triggering the redirect.

The cookie naming might be the culprit here. With `NEXTAUTH_FORCE_SECURE_COOKIES=true`, NextAuth prefixes the cookie as `__Secure-next-auth.session-token`, but if the home page redirect logic is checking for the unprefixed `next-auth.session-token` instead, it would fail to find the session and bounce the user back to login.

I'm tracing through how `getServerSession` retrieves the JWT token—it uses `getToken` from next-auth which reads from cookies and parses them, defaulting to the `next-auth.session-token` cookie (or the secure variant if that's enabled). The function auto-detects the secure cookie setting by checking whether the NEXTAUTH_URL starts with https.

Since NEXTAUTH_URL is set to `http://localhost:8080`, `getToken` defaults to looking for the non-secure cookie name, but the actual cookie was set with the `__Secure-` prefix because `NEXTAUTH_FORCE_SECURE_COOKIES=true`. This mismatch is why the session endpoint works—it uses NextAuth's internal session callback which knows the real cookie name—but `getServerSession` fails to find the token.

The fix is to either pass `secureCookie: true` or specify the `cookieName` directly to `getToken`, switch NEXTAUTH_URL to https, or disable the force secure cookies setting. Let me check what parameters are being passed to `getToken` in the current code. content.text:
Found the root cause:

- The Alloy compose sets `NEXTAUTH_FORCE_SECURE_COOKIES=true`, which makes NextAuth write cookies with the `__Secure-` prefix (e.g. `__Secure-next-auth.session-token`).
- `getServerSession.ts` reads the JWT via `getToken({ req, secret })` but does **not** pass `secureCookie`. With `NEXTAUTH_URL=http://localhost:8080`, `getToken` defaults to looking for `next-auth.session-token` (no prefix) — so it never finds the real cookie.
- Result: `/api/auth/session` works (NextAuth handler knows the real cookie name), but the home page's server-side session check fails → it redirects to `/auth/login`.

The fix is to make `getToken()` honor the same `NEXTAUTH_FORCE_SECURE_COOKIES` env var that the cookie-write path already honors.

content.text:
Login now works — landed on `/event-types` as `pro`.

## What was happening

The Alloy compose sets `NEXTAUTH_FORCE_SECURE_COOKIES=true` because the Alloy preview embeds the app in an HTTPS cross-site iframe, which requires `SameSite=None; Secure` cookies. NextAuth honors that flag on the **write** side via `defaultCookies()` in `packages/lib/default-cookies.ts`, prefixing the session cookie with `__Secure-` (so the cookie is named `__Secure-next-auth.session-token`).

But on the **read** side, the codebase's two server-side helpers — `packages/features/auth/lib/getServerSession.ts` and `packages/features/auth/lib/getLocale.ts` — call `getToken({ req, secret })` without passing `secureCookie`. With `NEXTAUTH_URL=http://localhost:8080` (not HTTPS), `getToken` defaults to looking for the unprefixed `next-auth.session-token` and never finds the real cookie.

Net effect: `/api/auth/session` (which uses NextAuth's own handler with the configured cookie name) worked fine and returned the user, but the home page's `getServerSession()` returned `null`, and `apps/web/app/page.tsx` redirected to `/auth/login` — the bounce-back loop you saw.

## The fix

Made the read path mirror the write path. Both `getServerSession.ts` and `getLocale.ts` now compute:

```ts
const useSecureCookies =
  process.env.NEXTAUTH_URL?.startsWith("https://") === true ||
  process.env.NEXTAUTH_FORCE_SECURE_COOKIES === "true";
```

…and pass `secureCookie: useSecureCookies` to `getToken()`. This is in line with the same env var the cookie-write path already respects via `defaultCookies()`.

You should be able to sign in with `pro` / `pro` (or `free` / `free`) now.
